channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [USHAMART] Usha Martin Board Recommends ₹3.75/Share Dividend for FY26
item.description:
Usha Martin Ltd's Board of Directors approved the standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. The statutory auditors, S R Batliboi & Co. LLP, issued an unmodified opinion on these results. The Board also recommended a dividend of ₹3.75 per equity share (375%) for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, which is subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming Annual General Meeting. Additionally, M/s Mani & Co. were appointed as Cost Auditor and M/s. Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu India LLP as Internal Auditor for FY2026-27.

  • Board approved Q4 and FY26 standalone and consolidated financial results.
  • Recommended ₹3.75 per share (375%) dividend for FY26.
  • Statutory auditors issued an unmodified opinion on financial results.
  • Mani & Co. appointed as Cost Auditor for FY2026-27.
  • Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu India LLP appointed as Internal Auditor for FY2026-27.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: USHA MARTIN LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [SPINAROO] Spinaroo Board to meet May 6 for Q4 FY26 results & investment
item.description:
Spinaroo Commercial Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors will convene on May 6, 2026. The key agenda items for the meeting include the consideration and approval of the Standalone Audited Financial Results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. Additionally, the Board will discuss and approve an investment in Spino Poly Products Private Limited, a sister company. The trading window for the company's securities remains closed until 48 hours after the unpublished price sensitive information is submitted to the stock exchanges, in accordance with the company's Code of Conduct.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 6, 2026.
  • To consider and approve Q4 FY26 standalone audited financial results.
  • To approve investment in sister company, Spino Poly Products Private Limited.
  • Trading window remains closed until 48 hours post-disclosure.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Spinaroo Commercial Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SENCO] Senco Gold: Melorra Acquisition Completion Delayed to June 30, 2026
item.description:
Senco Gold Ltd. announced an update regarding its acquisition of August Jewellery Private Limited (Melorra). The company had previously announced the acquisition with an expected completion date of April 30, 2026. Due to ongoing transaction processes, the completion date is now expected to be June 30, 2026. All other terms and conditions of the acquisition remain the same as previously disclosed on January 21, 2026.

  • Senco Gold's acquisition of Melorra is now expected to complete by June 30, 2026.
  • The delay is due to ongoing transaction-related processes.
  • All other terms and conditions of the acquisition remain unchanged.
  • Initial acquisition details were disclosed on January 21, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Senco Gold Ltd | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [HNDFDS] Hindustan Foods Commences Commercial Operations at Panipat Ice Cream Facility
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Hindustan Foods Limited (HFL) has started commercial operations at its new ice cream manufacturing facility in Panipat, Haryana. This is HFL's third ice cream unit in India, expanding its presence in the segment. The plant is currently ramping up, with utilization expected to increase gradually over the next few quarters. The company highlighted the Indian ice cream market's steady growth (12-15% CAGR, ₹35,000 crore market size) and favorable demand outlook due to low penetration, rising incomes, and expanding e-commerce channels. The facility was commissioned in approximately 10 months.

  • HFL commenced operations at its third ice cream manufacturing unit in Panipat, Haryana.
  • The new facility expands HFL's presence in the growing Indian ice cream market.
  • Utilization is expected to scale up gradually over the coming quarters.
  • The Indian ice cream market is projected to grow at a 12-15% CAGR.
  • HFL is now well-positioned with three ice cream plants and backward integration.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HINDUSTAN FOODS LTD. | Category: Expansion

item.title: [HNDFDS] Hindustan Foods begins ice-cream production at new Panipat facility
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Hindustan Foods Ltd has announced the commencement of commercial production of ice-creams at its new manufacturing facility located in Panipat, Haryana. This development is in compliance with Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. The new facility is expected to enhance the company's production capabilities and market presence in the ice-cream segment, contributing to its overall business growth.

  • Hindustan Foods commenced commercial ice-cream production.
  • New manufacturing facility is located in Panipat, Haryana.
  • Enhances company's production capacity for ice-creams.
  • Signals business expansion in the food processing sector.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HINDUSTAN FOODS LTD. | Category: Expansion

item.title: [GCSL] Gretex Board to meet May 7, 2026 for Q4/FY26 results
item.description:
Gretex Corporate Services Ltd. has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting for Thursday, May 07, 2026. The primary agenda is to consider and approve the Audited Financial Results, both Standalone and Consolidated, for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. Additionally, the company reiterated that its trading window for dealing in securities remains closed from April 01, 2026, for all Directors, Key Managerial Personnel, Designated Persons, and their immediate relatives. This closure will continue until 48 hours after the declaration of the financial results, in compliance with SEBI's Insider Trading Regulations.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 7, 2026.
  • To approve Q4 and FY26 audited financial results.
  • Results will include both standalone and consolidated figures.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026, until results declaration.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Gretex Corporate Services Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [KOTIC] Kothari Industrial launches new FMCG & Vending Division, 4 brands
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Kothari Industrial Corporation Ltd (KICL) has officially launched its new FMCG & Vending Division, marking a strategic business expansion into high-growth consumer segments. The company also inaugurated its facility and process centre in Maraimalai Nagar, Tamil Nadu. This initiative signifies KICL's entry into the fast-growing FMCG and smart vending ecosystem. Key brand launches include "VENDIKO" for smart vending solutions (juices, ready-to-eat food), "CHUSIP" for non-carbonated fruit beverages (including nata de coco variants), "NABAKO" for premium baked packaged products (cookies, cakes), and "THE CRAFTED CIRCLE" for ethnic snacks and traditional sweets. The launch, effective April 30, 2026, is initially focused on the domestic Indian market, targeting major metropolitan cities and urban transit locations. This expansion aims to build a future-ready FMCG portfolio and leverage technology-driven distribution channels.

  • KICL launched a new FMCG & Vending Division for strategic business expansion.
  • Inaugurated a new facility and process centre in Maraimalai Nagar, Tamil Nadu.
  • Introduced four new brands: VENDIKO, CHUSIP, NABAKO, and THE CRAFTED CIRCLE.
  • Focuses on smart vending solutions, non-carbonated beverages, baked goods, and ethnic snacks.
  • Initial launch targets the domestic Indian market across major metropolitan cities.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: KOTHARI INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION LTD. | Category: New Launch

item.title: [USHAMART] Usha Martin Board Approves FY26 Results, Recommends ₹3.75 Dividend
item.description:
Usha Martin Ltd's Board of Directors approved the standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. The statutory auditors, S R Batliboi & Co. LLP, issued an unmodified opinion on the financial results. The Board also recommended a dividend of ₹3.75 per equity share (375%) for FY26, subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming AGM. Additionally, M/s Mani & Co. were appointed as Cost Auditor and M/s. Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu India LLP as Internal Auditor for the financial year 2026-27.

  • Board approved audited financial results for Q4 and FY26.
  • Recommended a dividend of ₹3.75 per equity share (375%) for FY26.
  • Statutory auditors issued an unmodified opinion on financial results.
  • Appointed M/s Mani & Co. as Cost Auditor for FY26-27.
  • Appointed M/s. Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu India LLP as Internal Auditor for FY26-27.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: USHA MARTIN LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SAMPAT] Sampat Aluminium clarifies share price movement is market-driven
item.description:
Sampat Aluminium Ltd. responded to a stock exchange query regarding significant share price movement. The company clarified that the movement is purely market-driven and not due to any undisclosed price-sensitive information or impending corporate action. It affirmed its compliance with SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, and its commitment to promptly informing the stock exchange of all relevant events. The management stated it is not connected with the price movement and will continue to make necessary disclosures, assuring investors of adherence to compliance requirements.

  • Company clarified significant share price movement.
  • States movement is purely market-driven.
  • No undisclosed price-sensitive information exists.
  • Confirms compliance with SEBI (LODR) Regulations.
  • Management not connected to price fluctuations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sampat Aluminium Ltd | Category: Clarification

item.title: [CYIENTDLM] Cyient DLM: VP & Head of Operations-India Mujeeb Rahiman resigns
item.description:
Cyient DLM Ltd has announced the resignation of Mr. Mujeeb Rahiman from his position as Vice President and Head of Operations-India. He will also cease to be a Senior Managerial Personnel of the company. The resignation is effective from the close of business hours on April 30, 2026. The company has informed the stock exchanges about this change in compliance with Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Mr. Mujeeb Rahiman, VP & Head of Operations-India, has resigned.
  • His cessation as Senior Managerial Personnel is effective April 30, 2026.
  • The company informed the exchanges as per SEBI regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Cyient DLM Ltd | Category: Management Change
